reasoning: There are currently no vaccines available for Hepatitis C, and vaccine development has faced high scientific hurdles over the last 30 years. Recent searches confirm that there are no HCV vaccines in late-stage (Phase 3) trials, and no impending approvals are expected in 2025. Standard of care relies heavily on Direct-Acting Antivirals (DAAs) which cure the vast majority of cases, reducing the immediate commercial push for a vaccine breakthrough. The only feasible way this question resolves 'Yes' is if there is unresolved Wikipedia vandalism precisely on one of the check dates, or an unannounced, universally surprising emergency rollout. Because such events are exceedingly rare, the probability remains near the minimum limit. I scale the probabilities very slightly up for the later dates solely to apply the principle of increasing uncertainty with time horizon.
